vpspaceman.caption: Linkin Park in Bangalore #vpspaceman #linkinpark

#tigerphotography #tiger #wildlife #tigersofindia #tigers 

A reminder to protect the wild. With only a few thousand left, every moment with them is a blessing. 🌍🧡

Linkin Park wasn’t just a band — they were a *gateway*.
The first time those distorted synths slammed into rap verses and raw emotion, something rewired in my brain.

They made techno feel angry, rap feel vulnerable, and rock feel futuristic.
Before playlists, before algorithms — LP taught a whole generation how genres could *collide* and still make sense.

For millennials, this wasn’t background music.
This was headphones on, volume maxed, feeling-seen-at-2AM music.
From **Hybrid Theory** to **Meteora**, they walked so today’s electronic, rap, and alt scenes could run.

If you ever screamed the chorus, nodded through the verses, or got pulled into techno and rap because of them — you know.
Linkin Park forever shaped the sound of our chaos. 🖤❤️🥰

#LinkinPark #MillennialAnthem #HybridTheory #Meteora #TechnoRoots RapRock ForeverLP

✨🌸 Celebrated **Navratri** in the most beautiful and peaceful way — with temple visits, divine blessings, and moments that truly touched my soul. 🙏
Feeling so grateful to witness the beauty of devotion, the glow of diyas, and the strength of Maa Durga’s presence all around. 💫🌺

There’s something so special about dressing up in festive colors, offering prayers, and just pausing to feel the divine energy.
My heart is full 💖

Thayambaka - Thayambaka is a ritual temple art that is often performed at annual festivals, but it can also be performed outside of temples at proscenium stages, open fields, or pageantry grounds. 
The structure of a thayambaka recital is thought to have developed from a combination of the classical Carnatic music of Tamil Nadu and the percussion tradition of central Kerala. 
The main instrument in a thayambaka is the chenda, a drum made from the soft wood of the jackfruit tree. The chenda is often used to accompany other forms of dance and rituals in Kerala, such as Kathakali, Koodiyattam, and Kannyar Kali.
